meta.title: The D Major Chord
meta.subtitle: Learn the triangle-shaped D chord—your third chord!

objectives: Learn the D major chord shape
Make a triangle pattern with your fingers

steps.title: Your third chord!
steps.body: You know E minor and A major—now let's add D MAJOR! This chord is special because your fingers make a little triangle shape. It's also the brightest, happiest-sounding chord you've learned so far. And guess what? You only strum 4 strings!

steps.title: The D major chord diagram
steps.body: Here's what D major looks like. Your three fingers make a triangle pointing down! Notice the X's on the two thickest strings? That means we DON'T play those strings—only the bottom 4 strings get strummed.

steps.title: Place your first finger
steps.body: Put your POINTER finger (1st finger) on the 2nd fret of the 3rd string (G string). This is the top point of your triangle!

steps.title: Add your second finger
steps.body: Put your RING finger (3rd finger) on the 3rd fret of the 2nd string (B string). This is the bottom-right point of your triangle. Notice it's one fret higher than your pointer finger!

steps.title: Add your third finger
steps.body: Put your MIDDLE finger (2nd finger) on the 2nd fret of the 1st string (high E string—the thinnest one). This is the bottom-left point of your triangle. Now you have a complete triangle shape!

steps.title: Check your triangle
steps.body: Look at your hand—your fingers should form a triangle pattern. Pointer on top, middle and ring fingers below. Make sure each finger is curved and pressing firmly behind the fret. The two thickest strings should be untouched.

steps.title: Strum only 4 strings
steps.body: Here's the tricky part: Start your strum from the 4th string (D string), NOT from the top. Strum down across only 4 strings. Try to avoid hitting those two thick strings—they'll make the chord sound muddy!

steps.title: Practice D major
steps.body: Strum your D major chord a few times. Listen for a bright, clear, happy sound. If it sounds buzzy, check that your fingers are pressing firmly and not touching other strings. This chord takes practice to get clean!

steps.title: 🎸 Three chords unlocked!
steps.body: Fantastic! You now know THREE guitar chords—Em, A, and D! 🎉 Do you know what that means? You can play THOUSANDS of songs! Most popular music uses just these three chords. You're not a beginner anymore—you're becoming a real guitarist! Keep practicing that triangle shape! 🌟
